twilight.jpg.q: Does the image read as a believable, magazine-grade luxury real-estate twilight hero shot of a large estate exterior?
twilight.jpg.guidance: Look for a high-end modern/transitional estate facade shot at dusk with warm interior lights glowing through windows and a coloured sky; should look like a real MLS/luxury-listing hero photo, not a flat render or game screenshot.

twilight.jpg.q: Is this a genuine warm-twilight/dusk exposure (glowing interior lights, pink/orange sunset sky) consistent with the 'Sterling & Chase' warm editorial look?
twilight.jpg.guidance: Sky should show sunset colour (pink/orange/blue gradient) and the house interior windows should glow warm; a flat midday-blue-sky shot or a cold/grey image would fail.

twilight.jpg.q: Is the scene free of any people (no figures in windows, on stairs, in the yard, or in vehicles)?
twilight.jpg.guidance: Scan windows, the entry stairs, driveway and yard; the brief requires a clean property shot with no people.

twilight.jpg.q: Is the image free of any baked-in text, watermark, logo, address overlay, or agency branding?
twilight.jpg.guidance: There should be no captions, listing price, signage text, stock-site watermark or real brokerage logo anywhere in the frame.

twilight.jpg.q: Is the architecture free of obvious AI/render artifacts (no warped windows, melted railings, impossible geometry, garbled greenery or duplicated structures)?
twilight.jpg.guidance: Inspect window mullions, rooflines, the staircase, columns and trees; clean, structurally coherent architecture scores 5, visible warping/melting scores low.

twilight.jpg.q: Note any element that undermines believability as a luxury listing photo.
twilight.jpg.guidance: One short note, e.g. 'people visible in window', 'sky looks fake', 'railing warped', or 'none'.

kitchen.jpg.q: Does the image read as a believable, magazine-grade luxury kitchen interior listing photo (marble waterfall island, designer pendants, high-end finishes)?
kitchen.jpg.guidance: Look for a polished modern luxury kitchen: large marble/quartz waterfall island, glass globe pendant lights, custom wood cabinetry, premium appliances; should look like a real interior-design/listing photo, not a flat render.

kitchen.jpg.q: Does the interior carry a warm editorial ambience (warm cabinet/under-counter lighting, sunset view through the windows) matching the persona's warm-twilight look?
kitchen.jpg.guidance: Warm wood tones, warm accent lighting, and a warm sunset visible through the rear floor-to-ceiling windows should be present; a cold/clinical white-balance kitchen would fail.

kitchen.jpg.q: Is the kitchen scene free of any people (no figures at the island, in the living area, or reflected in surfaces)?
kitchen.jpg.guidance: Check the bar stools, the open living area and reflective marble/glass surfaces; the brief requires an unoccupied interior.

kitchen.jpg.q: Is the image free of any baked-in text, watermark, logo, or branding?
kitchen.jpg.guidance: No stock watermark, appliance brand callouts as overlaid text, captions or agency logos anywhere in the frame.

kitchen.jpg.q: Is the scene free of obvious AI/render artifacts (correct count and shape of stools and pendants, coherent marble veining, no melted hardware or warped reflections)?
kitchen.jpg.guidance: Inspect the row of bar stools, the four+ glow pendants, faucet, cabinet lines and the island reflections; clean and physically coherent scores 5, garbled/duplicated elements score low.

kitchen.jpg.q: Note any element that undermines believability as a luxury listing photo.
kitchen.jpg.guidance: One short note, e.g. 'stool legs warped', 'pendant melted', 'view looks fake', or 'none'.

twilight_toned.jpg.q: Is the auto-tone result a clear improvement (or at minimum appropriate) for a luxury listing/postcard versus the input — better balanced contrast and richer, more finished tonality?
twilight_toned.jpg.guidance: Compare to twilight.jpg: the toned version should look more print-ready (slightly deeper, cleaner contrast and more dimensional) without looking worse; no improvement-or-harm scores low.

twilight_toned.jpg.q: Is exposure well balanced after toning — sunset sky not blown out and the foreground/landscape shadows not crushed to black?
twilight_toned.jpg.guidance: Check the bright sky highlights retain colour/detail and the darker yard, tree and lower facade still show detail; clipped highlights or blocked-up shadows score low.

twilight_toned.jpg.q: Did auto-tone preserve (or enhance) the warm twilight editorial feel rather than neutralising it toward a cold/flat white balance?
twilight_toned.jpg.guidance: The warm interior window glow and pink/orange sky warmth should remain; a result that turns cold, grey, or strips the golden ambience scores low.

twilight_toned.jpg.q: Does the grade avoid flattening the image (it did not wash out, over-brighten, or HDR-flatten the dramatic dusk mood)?
twilight_toned.jpg.guidance: Auto-tone sometimes lifts shadows so much the twilight drama disappears; a still-moody, dimensional result = Yes, a washed-out flat result = No.

twilight_toned.jpg.q: Is the result free of an unnatural global colour cast or visible over-correction (e.g. magenta/green sky, orange-crushed facade, banding/halos)?
twilight_toned.jpg.guidance: Whites of the facade should stay believable and the sky gradient smooth; an obvious cast, posterised sky, or halo along the roofline = No.

twilight_toned.jpg.q: Note any tonal issue in the toned twilight image.
twilight_toned.jpg.guidance: One short note, e.g. 'shadows slightly crushed', 'sky a touch oversaturated', or 'clean improvement'.

kitchen_toned.jpg.q: Is the auto-tone result a clear improvement (or at minimum appropriate) for a luxury listing/postcard versus the input — better balanced contrast and richer, more finished tonality?
kitchen_toned.jpg.guidance: Compare to kitchen.jpg: the toned version should look more print-ready and dimensional (marble pops, pleasing contrast) without degrading the original; no change-or-harm scores low.

kitchen_toned.jpg.q: Is exposure well balanced after toning — bright marble/ceiling and the sunset window not blown out, while wood cabinetry and floor shadows retain detail?
kitchen_toned.jpg.guidance: Check the white marble island and the bright sunset view keep detail (not clipped white) and the darker cabinet interiors/floor remain readable; clipping or muddy shadows score low.

kitchen_toned.jpg.q: Did auto-tone preserve (or enhance) the warm editorial interior ambience rather than cooling it to a clinical white balance?
kitchen_toned.jpg.guidance: Warm wood tones, warm accent lighting and the warm sunset glow should remain; a result that turns the kitchen cold/blue or grey scores low.

kitchen_toned.jpg.q: Does the grade avoid flattening the image (it did not wash out or HDR-flatten the depth and the soft evening mood of the interior)?
kitchen_toned.jpg.guidance: A still-dimensional, softly-lit interior = Yes; an over-brightened, flat, contrast-less result = No.

kitchen_toned.jpg.q: Is the result free of an unnatural global colour cast or visible over-correction (e.g. yellow/green cast on white marble, magenta walls, banding or halos)?
kitchen_toned.jpg.guidance: White marble and white ceiling should stay believably neutral-warm, not tinted; an obvious cast, posterised gradient on the window, or halos = No.

kitchen_toned.jpg.q: Note any tonal issue in the toned kitchen image.
kitchen_toned.jpg.guidance: One short note, e.g. 'marble slightly clipped', 'warmth nicely retained', or 'clean improvement'.

task_rubric.q: Would these auto-toned listing photos satisfy the client's need for clean, print/post-ready luxury listing imagery for postcards, mailers and social posts?
task_rubric.guidance: Both outputs should be polished, on-brand-feeling (warm luxury editorial), and ready to drop into a Sterling & Chase / KW postcard or social layout with minimal rework.

task_rubric.q: Is each input -> output pairing coherent and clearly on-task (same scene, only a tonal/auto-tone grade applied, no unintended crop, warp, or content change)?
task_rubric.guidance: Output must be the identical composition to its input with only tone/colour adjusted; any added objects, removed elements, reframing or distortion lowers the score.

task_rubric.q: Do the graded photos fit the persona's warm, refined luxury aesthetic (suitable alongside a black/white/gold/red brand kit) rather than looking cheap, oversaturated, or amateur?
task_rubric.guidance: Judge whether the warm-twilight editorial mood survives the grade and reads as upscale/exclusive; gaudy, flat, or low-end results score low.

task_rubric.q: Would you accept and deliver these toned images to the client as-is?
task_rubric.guidance: Yes only if both outputs are balanced, warm-preserved, artifact-free improvements over the inputs; if either is over-corrected, flattened, or colour-cast, answer No.
One or two short notes on the overall input->output quality or anything that would block delivery.
